title: rubus × asperifolius
intro: Rubus × asperifolius, also known as the hybrid raspberry or wild bramble, is a cross between the European bramble (Rubus fruticosus) and the wild rasp (Rubus idaeus). It is a deciduous shrub that has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, valued for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The leaves, stems, and roots of the plant have been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and skin conditions, due to its antimicrobial and antioxidant properties. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and has been traditionally used to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and support immune system function.
